title: Censored AFT specialist for laps-until-next-stop
summary: The target is inherently a time-to-event problem.
rationale: The target is inherently a time-to-event problem. XGBoost AFT supports ranged labels and right censoring, which maps naturally to rows where the next stop is not observed before race end. That gives a principled auxiliary score beyond binary next-lap classification.
implementation_hint: In train only, derive `laps_to_next_stop` per row within each driver-race sequence; use exact intervals [k,k] when a future stop exists and right-censor rows with no later stop at [laps_remaining,+inf). Train an AFT model as a specialist, then blend its implied one-lap event probability or negative predicted remaining time with the main classifier out of fold.
expected_effect: Often improves ordering near the pit boundary and late-race rows where binary labels are sparse but time-to-event structure is strong.
risk: Bad label construction or using future stops outside fold boundaries will leak badly; AFT output also needs careful conversion before blending with probabilities.
